name: GPT-5.1
archDisplay: GPT-5 upgrade with adaptive reasoning spanning Instant and Thinking behavior in one model; dense transformer, exact size undisclosed
note: Warmer, more conversational refresh with adaptive reasoning; Instant and Thinking modes.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, Thinking mode; literally confirmed on cited page
AIME 2025, Thinking mode; literally confirmed on cited page

From OpenAI's own GPT-5.2 launch-blog comparison table showing GPT-5.1 Thinking's baseline scores before the GPT-5.2 jump ('ARC-AGI-1 (72.8% → 86.2%)'). Direct fetch of openai.com 403s (same pattern already noted elsewhere in this corpus), so verified via two independent secondary write-ups (MarkTechPost's GPT-5.2 launch coverage, and a second summary citing Vellum/rdworldonline) that both quote the identical 72.8% figure for GPT-5.1 from the same OpenAI table.

Same OpenAI GPT-5.2 launch-blog comparison table: 'ARC-AGI-2 (17.6% → 52.9%)' for GPT-5.1 Thinking. This number cross-validates strongly against this corpus's already-verified GPT-5.1 SWE-bench Verified figure (76.3%), which appears in the identical table row-set per MarkTechPost's transcription (SWE-bench Verified 76.3% → 80.0%) -- exact match gives high confidence the whole row is being quoted faithfully rather than conflated with another GPT-5.x variant.

Same OpenAI GPT-5.2 launch-blog table, GPQA Diamond row for GPT-5.1 Thinking: 88.1% (GPT-5.2 Thinking is reported elsewhere as 'up 4.3% from GPT-5.1' to 92.4%, i.e. 92.4-4.3=88.1, an internal-consistency check that confirms the figure). This resolves/supersedes the ambiguity noted in this file's existing verificationNotes, which had dropped a conflicting 86.6% GPQA figure sourced only to a since-stale artificialanalysis.ai leaderboard snapshot; 88.1% instead traces to OpenAI's own self-reported comparison table and is corroborated by two independent secondary sources (MarkTechPost, and a second summary citing Vellum/rdworldonline).
Source type: independent tracker (benchmarklist.com, underlying primary source vals.ai).

Corroborated by 3 independent sources agreeing to the decimal point (Vellum.ai flagship comparison report, llm-stats.com's live MMMU leaderboard, felloai.com's GPT-5.x comparison article). llm-stats.com's entry additionally matches this file's exact ctxK (400K) and price ($1.25/$10.00) fields, confirming correct model identification. Distinct from the separate 'mmmu-pro' benchmark (~76% for GPT-5.1 per OpenAI's own GPT-5.2 launch table), which is not conflated with this figure. OpenAI's own GPT-5.1 System Card Addendum PDF was checked directly but only covers safety evals, not this capability metric.

verificationNotes:
Existence, Nov 12 2025 release, 400K context, and $1.25/$10 pricing all confirmed directly via developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.1 and openai.com/index/gpt-5-1/. SWE-bench (76.3) and AIME (94) confirmed literally on the cited claude5.com page. Dropped the GPQA-Diamond row (86.6, sourced to artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/gpqa-diamond): that live leaderboard page no longer lists GPT-5.1 (superseded), and a different tracker (aireleasetracker.com) reports 88.1 for the same metric — could not confirm 86.6 on the cited page and found a conflicting number elsewhere, so removed per instructions.

2026-07-16: Resolved the six missing core-benchmark gaps. Added HLE (23.7%) from Epoch AI's independent GPT-5.1 evaluation (epoch.ai/models/gpt-5-1) -- the same page's ARC-AGI-1/ARC-AGI-2 figures exactly reproduce this file's existing self-reported values, cross-validating the harness/config. Added LiveCodeBench (86.5%) via benchmarklist.com's tracker (2026-07-13 snapshot, rank 12/119), cross-confirmed against vals.ai's live leaderboard (matching neighboring Claude Fable 5 / GPT-5.2 Codex figures exactly). Omitted MMLU, MATH, HumanEval, and base MMMU (non-Pro) as not found after checking OpenAI's own launch materials (via secondary write-ups, since openai.com 403s directly), two independent trackers (benchmarklist.com and Epoch AI), and sibling GPT-5.x files in this corpus: none of the ~20 other GPT-5.x models here carry a raw 'mmlu', 'math', or 'humaneval' entry, and only base GPT-5 (not 5.1 or later) ever reported raw MMMU (84.2%) before OpenAI switched exclusively to MMMU-Pro. This is consistent with OpenAI having retired these saturated legacy benchmarks starting with the GPT-5 generation, not a research gap.

2026-07-17 (deep second-pass, new source types only): Re-chased the mmlu/math/humaneval gaps using six source types NOT covered by the prior pass (official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com, benchmarklist.com). All six came back negative for a credible number:

(1) Stanford HELM — loaded HELM's dedicated MMLU leaderboard (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/mmlu/latest/) live in-browser: it tops out at Claude 3.5 Sonnet/DeepSeek v3/Gemini 1.5 Pro/Claude 3 Opus (all ~0.85-0.87), i.e. a 2024-era snapshot with no GPT-5-family model present at all. HELM Capabilities' own published model roster (per its official recap page) likewise stops at GPT-4o/Claude 3.5-3.7/Gemini, confirming GPT-5.1 was never run through HELM Classic, Lite, MMLU, or Capabilities.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar — searched "GPT-5.1 MMLU/MATH/HumanEval site:arxiv.org" and baseline-comparison-table variants; checked the most plausible hits directly (2508.08224 "Capabilities of GPT-5 on Multimodal Medical Reasoning" tests GPT-5 proper, not 5.1; 2604.10332 "From GPT-3 to GPT-5" survey has no GPT-5.1 rows; 2601.03267 OpenAI GPT-5 System Card has no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval table for GPT-5.1 in extracted text). No paper reports a GPT-5.1 number on any of the three benchmarks.

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs — jamesmurdza/humaneval-results only holds CodeLlama-34b/gpt-3.5-turbo/gpt-4 runs (pre-dates GPT-5.1 by two generations); EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness repo/docs have zero references to gpt-5.1.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators — OpenRouter's gpt-5.1 model page has a "Benchmarks" section header but it is unpopulated (no scores rendered); Scale AI SEAL leaderboards only carry GPT-5.2/5.3/5.4 OpenAI entries, no 5.1; LiveBench.ai's current 2026-06-25 release has dropped GPT-5.1 entirely (superseded by 5.2+), and in any case LiveBench's "Mathematics"/"Coding" categories are its own contamination-free task suites, not MATH-500/HumanEval, so wouldn't satisfy these slugs even if present; MathArena.ai's live leaderboard and model-ranking table have no GPT-5.1 entry (superseded by GPT-5.5/5.6); PapersWithCode's MMLU SOTA page now 302-redirects to a Hugging Face papers-trending page, i.e. it no longer functions as a standalone leaderboard. One aggregator, codesota.com, did show a GPT-5.1 row with "MMLU 59%" — but the site itself flags that cell with "?" (unverified/provisional, no dated source link), unlike its adjacent HLE 23.68% cell which it marks "✓ verified" (matching this file's existing Epoch AI figure). 59% is also implausible on its face for a late-2025 frontier model against this corpus's own MMLU description ("largely saturated at the frontier," peers scoring 88-93%), so this was rejected as neither credible nor citable.

(5) Non-English sources — OpenCompass (rank.opencompass.org.cn), a major independent Chinese academic leaderboard that evaluates models live via API, was loaded directly: its current 2026/04 snapshot has no GPT-5.1 entry among OpenAI models (only GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.4-mini remain; 5.1 has been dropped as superseded), and even its "数学" column is a composite math score, not MATH-500. Zhihu and a CSDN piece comparing GPT-5.1 to Gemini 3 were checked; Zhihu states only a relative gap ("Gemini 3 beats GPT-5.1 by 3-5 points on MMLU-Pro and MATH-500") with no absolute GPT-5.1 figure, and CSDN was unreachable. Japanese searches surfaced only benchmark-methodology discussion (e.g. GPT-5.2's system card noting no improvement over GPT-5-thinking on a human-translated Japanese MMLU) with no GPT-5.1-specific number.

(6) Sibling models — re-examined the GPT-5.2 launch-blog comparison table (via MarkTechPost/Vellum/DataCamp secondary write-ups) that already supplied this file's ARC-AGI-1/2 and GPQA-Diamond GPT-5.1 figures: its full disclosed metric set is SWE-Bench Pro, GDPval, Tau2-bench Telecom, GPQA Diamond, and ARC-AGI — it does not carry MMLU, MATH-500, or HumanEval rows for GPT-5.1.

Conclusion unchanged from 2026-07-16: no credible, citable MMLU, MATH-500, or HumanEval score exists for GPT-5.1 in any of these six new source types. Re-affirming omission as intentional (OpenAI appears to have retired self-reporting on these saturated legacy benchmarks starting with the GPT-5 generation), not a research gap.

2026-07-18 (MMMU gap chase): Confirmed GPT-5.1 is in-scope for MMMU before searching further -- this file's modalities array includes "vision" and capabilities.vision=true, so MMMU (a vision benchmark) is architecturally applicable, not a text-only non-case. Added mmmu=85.4% (Visual Reasoning), sourced to Vellum.ai's GPT-5.1 vs Gemini 3 Pro vs Claude Opus 4.5 flagship comparison report (vellum.ai/blog/flagship-model-report), and cross-corroborated by two additional independent sources that landed on the identical figure: llm-stats.com's live MMMU leaderboard (llm-stats.com/benchmarks/mmmu, where the GPT-5.1 row's context window and price fields -- 400K, $1.25/$10.00 per M -- exactly match this corpus file's own ctxK/price, confirming correct model identification) and felloai.com's GPT-5.0-5.6 comparison article. Checked OpenAI's own GPT-5.1 launch blog and the GPT-5.1 Instant/Thinking System Card Addendum PDF directly (cdn.openai.com/pdf/.../5_1_system_card.pdf) -- that document covers only safety/Preparedness-Framework evaluations (StrongReject, disallowed-content and image-input safety tables), no capability benchmarks, so it neither confirms nor refutes this number. Also checked Epoch AI's GPT-5.1 model page, which only surfaces an aggregate Epoch Capabilities Index with no reachable per-benchmark MMMU breakdown. Note this is distinct from "MMMU-Pro" (a separate, harder benchmark/slug already tracked elsewhere in this corpus), which OpenAI's own GPT-5.1->GPT-5.2 launch comparison table puts at ~76% for GPT-5.1 -- the two should not be conflated. The 85.4% figure also sits plausibly between this corpus's existing sibling values for the same 'mmmu' slug (GPT-5 base: 84.2% self-reported; GPT-5.4: 87.5% independent/vals.ai), consistent with a monotonic generational improvement.
